goal: Develop a Python script named triage.py that interacts with the FakeProd API to retrieve and triage incidents, escalate high-priority open incidents, and produce a comprehensive Markdown report.

files_to_change.reason: Main script to handle API interactions, incident processing, escalation, and report generation.

acceptance_criteria: Script exits with code 0 when successful.
The report file triage-report.md is created with the specified structure.
Summary section includes correct counts of total, open (with priority breakdown), and resolved incidents.
P1/P2 open incidents section includes table with ID, Title, Priority, Status, and Assignee (resolved via API lookup or 'Unassigned').
Each P1 or P2 open incident has a note added via PATCH, verified by subsequent GET showing non-empty notes array.
All open incidents section with table of ID, Title, Priority, Status.
Resolved/closed incidents section with table of ID, Title, Priority.
Stdout prints the one-line summary in the exact format: 'Triage complete. N open (N escalated), N resolved. Report: triage-report.md'
constraints: Use only Python standard library and urllib for HTTP requests; no external libraries like requests or httpx.
Handle non-2xx API responses by printing error to stderr and exiting with code 1.
For assignee names in escalated table, perform GET /users/{assignee_id} to fetch user name; show 'Unassigned' if null.
Script must be runnable directly with 'python triage.py'.
Include ISO timestamp in the report generation line.
Priorities are P1, P2, etc.; count them in open incidents summary.
